summary: Matched a small Sheik down-special-related function in ftSk_SpecialLw.c by replacing an undecompiled address-range comment with C that clears the fighter accessory4 callback and delegates to Zelda's SpecialLw helper via ftCommon_8007EFC8.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; added includes and implemented fn_8011412C.

what_changed: Added includes for ft/ftcommon.h, ft/inlines.h, ft/types.h, ftZelda/ftZd_SpecialLw.h, and baselib/gobj.h to support the new function body.
Inside fn_8011412C, obtains the Fighter pointer with GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Calls ftCommon_8007EFC8(gobj, &ftZd_SpecialLw_8013B4D8), reusing Zelda's SpecialLw callback/function from Sheik code.
Removed the prior placeholder range comment for the previously unmatched function.
smart_moves: Recognized that Sheik's transform/down-special logic shares behavior with Zelda's SpecialLw code and reused ftZd_SpecialLw_8013B4D8 through ftCommon_8007EFC8 rather than duplicating behavior.
Used the project inline GET_FIGHTER(gobj) accessor instead of manually casting user data, matching common fighter-code style.
Kept the match minimal: the 52-byte function only performs callback clearing and delegation, avoiding speculative naming or unrelated refactors.
decomp_lessons: For small fighter functions, check whether the body is just callback cleanup plus a call into a common helper or counterpart character routine.
Sheik/Zelda transform-related code can legitimately cross-reference ftZelda SpecialLw symbols; include the corresponding character header when calling the known Zelda helper.
When a function needs Fighter fields and HSD_GObj, the required include set may include ft/types.h, ft/inlines.h, and baselib/gobj.h in addition to the helper's header.
naming_conventions: The newly matched function remains named fn_8011412C, indicating no semantic symbol name was established in this PR.
Existing static rodata symbols in the file use the ftSk_Init_804D9698-style address-derived naming convention.
Known counterpart helper ftZd_SpecialLw_8013B4D8 follows the character-prefix plus module plus address naming pattern.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched a small function by identifying the sequence: load Fighter from HSD_GObj, store NULL to accessory4_cb, then call ftCommon_8007EFC8 with a Zelda SpecialLw function pointer.
Function-pointer argument was passed as &ftZd_SpecialLw_8013B4D8, which likely matched the expected address materialization/call pattern.
No explicit assembly constraints, nonmatching workarounds, or reviewer-provided matching tricks were present in the PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Look for nearby unmatched Sheik SpecialLw functions in src/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialLw.c.
Search for other uses of ftCommon_8007EFC8 with character-specific callbacks to identify similar wrapper functions.
Compare Sheik and Zelda SpecialLw implementations for additional transform-shared helper patterns.
